claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 17 years old (over 16), I need to ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** This would include things like:
- A-levels or equivalent
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to Level 3
- A course at college or sixth form

Or has Alex left education/training and registered with a careers service or the armed services?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex isn't in college or doing A-levels or anything like that. But they have recently started an apprenticeship - they're training in a trade.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is important information.

I have one more question to confirm the details:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship based in England?**

This matters because apprenticeships in England have specific rules regarding Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the apprenticeship is in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

I just need to check a couple more things about your circumstances:

**Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're here in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have a clear picture of your situation.

I just need to confirm one last thing:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all of that information. I've now been able to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

## Eligibility Outcome for Alex

**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

### Reason:

Although you meet many of the standard eligibility requirements (you live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex), there is a specific rule that affects your situation:

**Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England.**

Since Alex has recently started an apprenticeship in England, this means you are no longer entitled to receive Child Benefit for them, even though Alex is only 17 years old.

---

I'm sorry this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if they return to approved education or training), you may be able to make a new claim at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like me to help clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Although you live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the specific rule about apprenticeships in England means that eligibility for Child Benefit ends when a child begins such an apprenticeship. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 17-year-old child Alex. While they would normally meet the basic eligibility criteria (living in the UK, responsible for a child, child lives with them, no one else claiming), Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, regardless of the child's age or other circumstances.

Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

While they would normally meet the basic eligibility criteria (living in the UK, responsible for a child, child lives with them, no one else claiming), Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, regardless of the child's age or other circumstances.
